title: Stelis tabacina
intro: Stelis tabacina is a flowering plant in the orchid family, native to Central and South America, known for its unique and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Stelis tabacina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis tabacina is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to support cardiovascular health and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Stelis tabacina belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This epiphytic orchid is classified within the genus Stelis, which includes over 1,000 species of orchids. The genus Stelis is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which consists of small to medium-sized orchids. Further classification includes the family Orchidaceae within the order Asparagales, and then the class Liliopsida.
